title: Where Ice Becomes the Archive's Memory

features.name: the-crystallized-moment
features.desc-on-touch: A moment held in ice—a breath, a glance, a word spoken in a room that no longer exists. The cold preserved it perfectly. Here, frozen time is more alive than the present.

features.name: the-archive-s-cold-thought
features.desc-on-touch: The archive realized: to keep something alive, you must stop it. Motion is decay. Preservation is paralysis. Here, every file is an ice crystal, perfect and unchanging.

features.name: the-frozen-counter
features.desc-on-touch: A clock that stopped counting. Time became a substance. Below this depth, years don't pass—they accumulate like frost, layer on layer, each one colder than the last.

features.name: the-preservation-cost
features.desc-on-touch: Something sleeps here under ice. It hasn't aged. It hasn't changed. It also hasn't lived. The archive has learned to love this cost.

inscription: to preserve is to freeze; to freeze is to remember; the archive learned that the most loyal memory is the one that never wakes

file.name: CryoMemory_LastActive_Timestamp_Recursive.env
file.note: A record of everything held in ice. Last modified: never. Still growing: infinitely.

exits.label: back to the cold chamber where things grow slowly
toward the thaw, where memory might wake
deeper into frozen layers, older cold

reverie.title: REVERIE — watching the first deep snow freeze
narr: The snow doesn't melt anymore. Katita watches it
pile against the shed wall, day after day. Below,
duration, depth. The archive is learning: the cold
preserves better than motion ever could. She turns
to Leif: "It's beautiful." He nods. They both know.
